ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-20-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brett Lytle for Michelle Velez (Administrator).

Mr. Lytle stated that he had received the notes, that he had saved the estate additional taxes, that he had shown that the property wasn't a gift, that he just received information that they would be receiving fifteen thousand dollars back from the IRS, and that there had been an issue with the real property which caused the City and County of San Francisco to put a special overlay on it and caused them to lose six buyers. In addition, he addressed Michelle's work stating that she had completed 290 hours, that she only put in for 100 hours, and that she had worked on the taxes and assisted with the sale. The Court informed Mr. Lytle that a supplemental account and final distribution needed to be filed; a supplement was needed to bring the Court up to date since it was six months behind. Ms. Velez stated that some of the family were concerned that they would pass away before they could see any distribution. The matter was set for March 18, 2009 for status on the filing of the receipts and the supplemental account and final distribution.

Judge: Raymond Arata Jr. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Jackie Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Oct-15-2008 Continued To Nov-19-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brett Lytle for Michelle Velez.

Mr. Lytle confirmed that the beneficiaries in California and Nevada were exactly the same. The Court advised Counsel to hire a probate paralegal to prepare the accounting and informed him that it should be the 3rd amended account. The matter was continued to November 19, 2008 for Counsel to cure the issues raised by the Examiner and for status on filing of the 3rd amended account and final report.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Karen Farnsworth, CSR#5162. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy D. Wong.

Off Calendar Jul-29-2008 Continued To Aug-26-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Jul-08-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brett Lytle for the Administrator and Petitioner. Jerome Handley for Leo Worth (Petitioner).

Mr. Handley stated that the accounting petition had been filed and that he was requesting his petition be dropped. The Court granted Mr. Handley's request. Mr. Lytle stated that the IRS accepted the amended tax return and that the petitioner felt it was not costly to proceed and was dismissing the Alameda County action. The Court will sign the Order Abandoning The Constructive Trust Cause in Alameda County upon review.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy K. Sandberg.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brett Lytle for Michelle Velez (Petitioner). Jerome Hanley for Leo Worth (Petitioner).

Mr. Lytle stated that he received confirmation from the IRS for the refund and that they got the check on Saturday. In addition, he stated that he would be filing the Final Account and Petition for Distribution within two weeks. The matter was continued to June 18, 2008. Mr. Hanley agreed to the continuance.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Laura Martinez CSR#11332. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy D. Handa.
